slides.eyebrow: Barcelona AI Tinkerers · Structured Finance Hackathon 2026
slides.title: LoanWhiz
slides.subtitle: A Structured Finance Agent Framework — compile the deal, don't chat about it.

slides.title: Don't chat about the deal — compile it
slides.intro: Three concerns a chatbot collapses into one. Read once, execute deterministically, orchestrate with an agent.

bullets.text: Docling + section-routed Gemini 2.5 Pro produce structured JSON.
Every fact carries a verbatim source excerpt.
Waterfall, covenant monitor, projector — typed and deterministic.
Plans which primitives to call, then explains.
Every call carries an audit entry + confidence.

slides.title: Validated against a real ING deal — to the cent
slides.intro: Green Lion 2024-1: our engine re-runs the deal's own published Notes & Cash waterfall. No report distribution figures fed in — Class A interest is computed (balance × coupon × days/360).

metrics.label: reconciliation gap
metrics.sub: the report's own €0.69 rounding modelled, not fudged

slides.title: The same primitives across three jurisdictions
slides.note: The unmodified pipeline runs end-to-end on all five. Extraction completeness is honest — non-English prospectuses extract partially; we show that, we don't fake it.

slides.title: A capability matrix, not a claim
slides.body: GET /capability-matrix runs every primitive across every deal and renders it in the /showcase view: validated / ran / not-applicable, each cell carrying its real reason. Today: 1 validated, 9 ran, 15 not-applicable. N/A is shown as honest scope — never hidden, never coloured green.

cards.title: MCP server
cards.body: The 8 SF primitives exposed as a governed MCP server — typed I/O + the evidence pack — consumable on any MCP host.

cards.title: Deal = data, not code
cards.body: Add a deal by dropping JSON into deals.json; no Python. The interpreter executes the deal's extracted model, not hardcoded logic.

cards.title: Open & portable
cards.body: Apache-2.0; CSV or parquet tapes; deeploans (Algoritmica's ESMA ETL) on the ingestion path with a direct fallback.

slides.kicker: Trust — FINOS AI Governance Framework + deeploans
slides.title: Governance is baked into the interface

cards.title: Audit trail
cards.body: Append-only entry per call: hashes, model version, timestamp, duration.

cards.title: Confidence scoring
cards.body: Mandatory on every primitive — coverage · cross-ref · self-assessment.

cards.title: Verbatim citations
cards.body: Every fact grounded to a section + excerpt a reviewer can locate.

cards.title: Data provenance
cards.body: Each tape tagged deeploans vs direct, carried into the evidence pack.

cards.title: Human-review routing
cards.body: Confidence < 0.70 → flagged; output can't be used until signed off.

cards.title: Model-risk class
cards.body: Decision-support tier, zero autonomy — outputs are inputs, never actions.

cards.title: Deal Analytics
cards.body: Overview · Pool & Performance · Waterfall · Compliance · Projection — the per-deal analyst workflow over the extracted model.

cards.title: Platform & Governance
cards.body: Showcase · Validation · Framework · Governance — the cross-deal, trust & reusability layer that headlines the work.

slides.title: Reusable, trustworthy, broadly applicable
slides.body: SF-native primitives + a registry, packaged as an MCP — not a one-off script. Validated to the cent against a real ING deal, demonstrated across Dutch, Italian and Spanish RMBS, with citations, governance and data-provenance on every output. And honest about its limits — the capability matrix shows exactly what's proven.
